metadata.title: Card plugin: configurable sidebar cards with image upload
metadata.summary: Adds a Card tool that renders a configurable list of cards (image, title, subtitle, link) in a sidebar panel. Card data lives in mission config and rides the existing save/load path; the only new backend surface is an admin-gated image-upload route, exposed in the Configure UI through a new "upload" field type.

overview.purpose: Declares the Card tool to MMGIS: its icon, panel metadata, and the config-form schema mission admins fill in.
overview.background: MMGIS discovers tools by globbing src/essence/Tools/*/config.json (API/updateTools.js) — dropping this file in is the entire registration step, no central list to edit. This is the best file to read first: it defines what a "card" is and how admins author one.

keyPieces.name: metadata
keyPieces.description: Panel placement hints: vertical, left/right, modern-layout aware.

keyPieces.name: config.rows
keyPieces.description: The admin form — an objectarray of cards, each with image/title/subtitle/linkUrl fields.

explanations.title: Register the Card tool
explanations.what: Names the tool, points `paths.CardTool` at the runtime module, and sets panel metadata (vertical orientation, left/right positions, modern-layout support).
explanations.why: These fields are how MMGIS's tool loader and the modern panel layout know the tool exists and where it's allowed to dock. `hasVars: true` marks that its state lives in mission-config variables rather than a dedicated table.

explanations.title: Card form schema + new upload field
explanations.what: Defines the admin form as an `objectarray` of cards; the `image` sub-field uses `type: "upload"` while title/subtitle/linkUrl are plain `text`.
explanations.why: `objectarray` gives admins add/remove/reorder rows for free. The `image` field is the one genuinely new piece — `"upload"` is a field type that doesn't exist in the Configure SPA yet and is introduced by this PR (see Maker.js).
explanations.impact: The stored `image` value is just a mission-relative path string, identical in shape to a typed path — so an admin can still paste a URL/path instead of uploading, and old configs stay valid.

overview.purpose: Pure validation helpers for the upload route — kept dependency-free so they can be unit-tested without spinning up Express or busboy.

explanations.title: MIME allowlist, SVG excluded
explanations.what: Maps a small set of raster image MIME types to safe file extensions; anything else (including `image/svg+xml`) returns null.
explanations.why: Deriving the extension from a server-controlled allowlist — rather than trusting the client's filename — is what keeps a malicious upload from landing as, say, a `.html`. SVG is deliberately left out because it can carry inline script (stored-XSS) when served from the Missions origin.

explanations.title: Reject path-traversal mission names
explanations.what: Validates the `mission` query param: must be a non-empty string with no `..`, no slashes/backslashes, and not a pure-dot name.
explanations.why: The mission name becomes a directory segment in the write path, so an unchecked value could escape the Missions folder. The pure-dot guard (`.`, `..`) is the subtle one — those resolve to a real directory segment that would drop the upload in the Missions root instead of a mission folder.

overview.purpose: The one new backend route: accepts a multipart image upload and writes it under Missions/<mission>/CardPlugin/uploads/<uuid>.<ext>, returning the stored mission-relative path.
overview.background: This is the only new server surface in the PR — the card data itself rides the existing mission-config save/load path. busboy is already a repo dependency (used by the Datasets routes); files written under Missions/ are immediately web-served by scripts/server.js.

explanations.title: Validate mission before any work
explanations.what: Rejects the request with 400 if the `mission` query param fails `isValidMission`.
explanations.why: Fail closed before touching the filesystem — the mission name is about to be used to build a write path.

explanations.title: Create the mission dir on demand
explanations.what: Builds the mission directory path without first checking that the directory exists.
explanations.why: Missions imported via "Upload Config.JSON" never materialize a Missions/<mission>/ folder (only the "add mission" flow does). Rejecting on a missing dir would block uploads for those missions. Since the name is already traversal-checked and the route is admin-gated, creating the path on demand is safe.

explanations.title: Cap to one file, 5MB
explanations.what: Configures busboy with `files: 1` and a `fileSize` limit, wrapped in try/catch for malformed headers.
explanations.why: The size cap is enforced server-side (busboy fires a `limit` event mid-stream) so a client can't bypass it; capping to a single file keeps the handler logic simple.

explanations.title: Resolve extension or reject
explanations.what: On the file part, derives the extension from the MIME type; if unsupported, drains the stream (`file.resume()`) and returns 400.
explanations.why: Draining the rejected file is necessary — if you don't consume the stream, the request never finishes and the socket hangs.

explanations.title: Write under a UUID filename
explanations.what: Creates the uploads dir, generates a `crypto.randomUUID()` filename with the allowlisted extension, and pipes the upload to disk.
explanations.why: A server-generated UUID name means the client filename is never trusted or reflected — no collisions, no injection through the name, no overwrite of an existing file.

explanations.title: Clean up on oversize / write error
explanations.what: On the busboy `limit` event (file too big) or a write-stream error, destroys the stream, unlinks the partial file, and clears `savedRelPath`.
explanations.why: Nulling `savedRelPath` is what prevents the later success handler from returning a path to a half-written or already-deleted file.

explanations.title: Respond on flush, not on close
explanations.what: Sends the success response from the write stream's `finish` event rather than from busboy's `close`.
explanations.why: busboy `close` fires when the upload has been drained into the write buffer, not when bytes are actually on disk — responding there races the write and could hand the client a path to a partial (or, on a late error+unlink, missing) file. This was the bug fixed in the second commit.
explanations.impact: Subtle ordering invariant: any future refactor that moves the success response back to `close` reintroduces the race.

explanations.title: Handle the no-file case
explanations.what: On `close`, if no file part ever arrived, responds 400.
explanations.why: A successful upload already responded from `finish`; this covers the only remaining path where nothing else would ever reply and the request would hang.

overview.purpose: Wires the upload router into the app. MMGIS auto-loads any API/Backend/*/setup.js, so this is the backend equivalent of config.json's auto-discovery.

explanations.title: Mount route, admin-gated
explanations.what: Registers the router at `/api/cardplugin` behind `ensureAdmin()` plus the standard header/content-type middleware.
explanations.why: Uploading is a Configure-page action, so it matches the auth posture of the /api/configure routes — `ensureAdmin` requires an admin/lead session (permission 111/110) in every AUTH mode, not the `ensureUser`+`stopGuests` pairing the design doc originally sketched. AUTH=none only opens viewing, not this write route, so the uploader is always an admin/lead. Same gate as editing mission config, which is the right bar for writing files into a mission.

overview.purpose: Client-side helper that POSTs an image File to the upload route and returns the stored path. The thin network layer behind the upload field.

explanations.title: Resolve the API base
explanations.what: Computes the API origin the same way configure/src/core/calls.js does — localhost:8888 in dev, else `ROOT_PATH`.
explanations.why: Reuses the established convention so the upload call hits the same backend as every other Configure API call, including reverse-proxy deployments where ROOT_PATH is a sub-path.

explanations.title: POST the file, unwrap the path
explanations.what: Sends multipart form data with `credentials: 'include'`, then returns `data.path` only on a success response, otherwise throws the server's error message.
explanations.why: `credentials: 'include'` carries the admin session cookie the route requires. Surfacing the server's own message (rather than a generic one) is what lets the upload field show "Unsupported image type" etc. inline.

overview.purpose: The React component for the new `upload` config field: a file picker with a thumbnail preview, upload/replace/clear buttons, and an uploading state.
overview.background: Stays a pure controlled component — it never touches the config store directly. It reports results through onChange(path)/onError(msg) so Maker.js can wire those into the existing config plumbing.

explanations.title: Upload on file select
explanations.what: On selection, resets the input value, guards on a missing mission, then uploads and calls `onChange(path)` on success or `onError(message)` on failure — keeping the prior value either way.
explanations.why: Resetting `e.target.value` lets the admin re-pick the same file to retry. The mission guard catches the case where a mission hasn't been saved/selected yet, since the route needs one.

explanations.title: Preview the current value
explanations.what: Builds a preview URL: pass through absolute/data/root-relative values, otherwise prefix with `Missions/<mission>/`.
explanations.why: The stored value is a mission-relative path, so it has to be resolved against the mission folder to render — mirroring the same resolution the runtime tool does (buildCardData.resolveImageUrl).

explanations.title: Picker UI with live states
explanations.what: Renders the thumbnail, an Upload/Replace button that flips to "Uploading…" and disables while in flight, a Clear button, and a hidden file input restricted to the four allowed image types.
explanations.why: The `accept` attribute mirrors the server's allowlist so the OS picker filters to valid types up front, but it's only a hint — the server validation in validate.js is the real gate.

overview.purpose: The Configure SPA's field renderer — a big switch on field `type` (text, dropdown, objectarray, colorpicker…) that turns a config schema into form inputs.
overview.background: This is the integration point: adding a `case` here is what makes `type: "upload"` in any config.json actually render. It's also why the new field works inside an objectarray for free — each card row reuses this same renderer.

explanations.title: New "upload" field case
explanations.what: Adds `case "upload"` (and the UploadField import at line 34): resolves the API domain, passes the active mission and current value into UploadField, and routes its callbacks into the existing config plumbing.
explanations.why: `onChange` reuses the same `updateConfiguration(forceField || com.field, value, layer)` call every other field type uses, so the uploaded path persists and saves through the unchanged config flow — the upload field is additive, not a parallel save path. `onError` surfaces failures via the existing snackbar.
explanations.impact: The mission is read as `msv.missionFolderName || msv.mission`; if neither is set (unsaved mission) the upload field's own guard handles it with an inline error rather than a bad request.

overview.purpose: The Configure SPA's static catalog of every tool and its config schema. The minified counterpart to the runtime config.json files.
overview.background: This file is a single minified line, so the diff shows the whole line changed — but the only addition is a new "Card" entry. The viewer can't pretty-print it; the meaningful change is the inserted Card object.

explanations.title: Add Card to the catalog
explanations.what: Inserts a "Card" entry mirroring src/essence/Tools/Card/config.json — same icon, metadata, and the cards objectarray schema with the `upload` image field.
explanations.why: The Configure SPA reads tools from this bundled catalog rather than globbing the filesystem, so the tool must be listed here for admins to see and configure it. The duplication with config.json is the existing convention, not something this PR introduces.

overview.purpose: The tool lifecycle object MMGIS calls into: initialize / make(targetId) / destroy / getUrlString. Mounts the React app into the panel.
overview.background: Follows the modern LayerManager tool pattern — a plain lifecycle object that talks to MMGIS only through the window.mmgisAPI event bus and mounts React via createRoot, rather than the older jQuery-style tools.

explanations.title: Widen panel on mobile
explanations.what: Asks the bus `app:isMobile`; if so, switches to full width and a fixed height. Failures are caught and logged, not thrown.
explanations.why: Requesting capabilities through the bus keeps the tool decoupled from MMGIS internals (no direct imports). The try/catch matters because the handler may not be registered yet during boot — a missing capability shouldn't break tool init.

explanations.title: Mount React into the panel
explanations.what: Resolves the target container (defaulting to `toolPanel`), creates a React root, and renders the adapter.
explanations.why: createRoot is the React 19 mounting API the modern tools use. The comment block documents a deliberate choice: it does NOT paint the classic dark panel background, because in a themed panel that dark color would bleed through the card gaps as an ugly frame.

explanations.title: Unmount on destroy
explanations.what: Unmounts the React root and clears state.
explanations.why: Without the explicit `unmount`, the React tree and its bus subscriptions would leak each time the tool panel closes and reopens.

overview.purpose: Bridges the MMGIS event bus to the presentational components: fetches card data and feeds it to <CardList>.
overview.background: Keeps all bus interaction in this one "adapter" component so the lib/ components stay pure and testable. The interesting problem it solves is timing — the data source isn't ready at mount.

explanations.title: Wait for the bus, then fetch
explanations.what: Defines a `refresh` that pulls cards via getCardData (clearing to empty on error), and gates the first fetch on `useMMGISHandlerReady('tool:getVars', refresh)`.
explanations.why: `tool:getVars` is only registered by Layers_.fina() partway through mission load. Fetching at mount would get an empty list and never recover, because nothing emits an event when vars first appear. Polling for the handler is the workaround for that one-shot readiness gap.
explanations.impact: Data is fetched once when the handler appears — there's no live refresh if the config's cards change at runtime. Fine for admin-authored content, but worth knowing.

overview.purpose: Orchestrator: reads the configured cards and the mission path off the bus, then hands both to the pure buildCardData transform.

explanations.title: Gather bus data, delegate transform
explanations.what: Requests `tool:getVars` (scoped to 'card') and `app:getMissionPath`, then calls buildCardData with defensive fallbacks.
explanations.why: Splitting the impure bus reads (here) from the pure transform (buildCardData) is what lets the mapping logic be unit-tested without mocking the whole bus — see resolveImageUrl.spec.js.

overview.purpose: Pure functions that turn raw config cards into render-ready props, resolving each stored image path into a usable URL.

explanations.title: Resolve a stored image path
explanations.what: Returns absolute/data/root-relative image values unchanged; prefixes everything else with the mission path.
explanations.why: Stored values are mission-relative (e.g. `CardPlugin/uploads/x.png`) and need the `Missions/<mission>/` prefix to load, but admins can also paste a full URL — the regex passthrough preserves backward compatibility with typed paths. This mirrors the preview logic in UploadField.

explanations.title: Map raw cards to props
explanations.what: Defensively returns [] for non-array input, otherwise maps each raw card to a CardItem with its resolved imageUrl.
explanations.why: Tolerating malformed/missing input here keeps a bad config from crashing the panel — the tool degrades to an empty list instead.

overview.purpose: Thin, typed wrappers around window.mmgisAPI (request/on/emit/provide/hasHandler) so the rest of the tool has a typed, null-safe bus interface.
overview.background: Same shape as LayerManager's bus wrappers. Every wrapper degrades gracefully when the API (or a method) isn't present, returning null/no-op instead of throwing.

explanations.title: Null-safe bus wrappers
explanations.what: Each function checks the API/method exists before calling, returning null (request), a no-op cleanup (on/provide), or false (hasHandler) otherwise.
explanations.why: The tool can load before mmgisAPI is fully populated, so guarding every access avoids crashes during boot and lets callers like useMMGISHandlerReady poll safely.

overview.purpose: A React hook that polls mmgisAPI.hasHandler(name) until a bus capability is registered, then fires onReady once.
overview.background: Exists because several MMGIS capabilities (like tool:getVars) are registered asynchronously during boot, with no event to subscribe to when they become available.

explanations.title: Poll until the handler exists
explanations.what: Fires immediately if the handler is already present; otherwise polls on an interval (default 200ms) until it appears or a timeout (default 10s) elapses, warning on timeout. Cleans up the interval on unmount.
explanations.why: Polling is the pragmatic fix for a missing readiness event. The timeout prevents a permanently-absent handler from polling forever, and the cleanup prevents a setInterval leak if the tool closes mid-wait.

overview.purpose: The CardItem shape shared between the transform and the presentational components — image already resolved to a URL.
Pure presentational component for one card: an image, title, and subtitle wrapped in a link. No bus, no data fetching.

explanations.title: Render one linked card
explanations.what: Returns null if there's neither title nor image; otherwise renders the whole card as an `<a target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">`, falling back to an empty image div when there's no imageUrl.
explanations.why: `rel="noopener noreferrer"` is the standard guard for `target="_blank"` links (prevents the opened page from reaching back via window.opener). The empty-div fallback keeps the card's layout stable when an image is missing rather than showing a broken-image icon.

overview.purpose: Pure presentational list: renders a <Card> per item, suppressing output until the first load resolves.

explanations.title: Render the card list
explanations.what: Returns null while `loading`, otherwise maps each card to a <Card>.
explanations.why: The loading guard avoids a flash of an empty panel before the first bus fetch resolves (recall the data arrives asynchronously after the handler is ready).

overview.purpose: Barrel export for the lib components plus a side-effect import of the compiled styles.

explanations.title: Pull in styles as a side effect
explanations.what: Imports index.scss for its side effects so the component CSS ships whenever the lib is used.
explanations.why: Co-locating the style import with the component barrel means consumers get styling automatically without a separate import.

overview.purpose: Style entrypoint that forwards the component partials. Theming tokens come from MMGIS's runtime per-theme CSS bundles.
Aggregates the card and card-list partials.

overview.purpose: Styles for a single card, driven by --theme-* custom properties with literal fallbacks.

explanations.title: Square-crop the image via CSS
explanations.what: Fixes the image to 65×65 with `object-fit: cover`.
explanations.why: This is the "automatic crop" — the design chose CSS cropping over server-side resize (called out as out-of-scope in the spec), so any aspect ratio renders as a clean square without backend image processing.

overview.purpose: Vertical flex layout and spacing for the list of cards.
Ambient module declaration so TypeScript accepts the `import './index.scss'` side-effect import.

files.newContent: declare module '*.scss'

overview.purpose: Unit tests for the pure backend validators (extensionForMime, isValidMission).

explanations.title: Cover the security guards
explanations.what: Asserts allowed MIME types map to extensions, SVG/PDF/undefined are rejected, plain mission names pass, and traversal/separator/dot/non-string names fail.
explanations.why: These are the security-critical branches (path traversal, SVG exclusion), so they're worth pinning with direct unit tests rather than only exercising them through the route.

overview.purpose: Unit tests for the pure frontend transform (resolveImageUrl, buildCardData).

explanations.title: Cover URL resolution + mapping
explanations.what: Checks empty/undefined input, absolute/data/root-relative passthrough, mission-relative prefixing, null mission tolerance, and the full raw-card-to-CardItem mapping.
explanations.why: Confirms the backward-compat passthrough (typed URLs still work) and the defensive empty-array behavior — the two things most likely to regress if the transform is touched.

overview.purpose: Unit tests for the client upload helper (uploadCardImage), with fetch/FormData stubbed.

explanations.title: Verify request shape + error path
explanations.what: Asserts the helper POSTs to the right URL with the mission query param and returns the path on success, and throws the server's message on a failure response.
explanations.why: Locks in the contract the UploadField relies on — correct URL/method and that server error messages propagate so they can be shown inline.
